text.title: API-First Development Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every API-First Development application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Spec MUST be OpenAPI 3.1 (not 3.0); 3.0 is rejected for new APIs.
rule.rationale: 3.1 aligns with JSON Schema 2020-12; 3.0 is a maintenance dead-end.

rule.statement: Spec MUST be merged before implementation PR opens.
rule.rationale: Implementation-first specs lie within two sprints.

rule.statement: Prism (or equivalent) mock server MUST be running before client work starts.
rule.rationale: Clients blocked on real API force tight coupling and timing bugs.

rule.statement: schemathesis / dredd contract tests MUST run in CI on every PR.
rule.rationale: Without CI gating, drift accumulates silently.

rule.statement: Any breaking change MUST bump the major version and ship under /v{N+1}.
rule.rationale: Silent breaking changes destroy client trust.
